location.office_time_required: 3 days per week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

seniority.level: Senior Manager / Group PM (people manager, 6+ years PM)
level_signals: 6+ years of experience in product management
2+ years of experience managing product managers
Manages a team of PMs and leads a large cross-functional team
Comp range $176K-$220K base, consistent with senior group PM level

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years of experience in product management, product design or equivalent consultant experience with significant time spent leading consumer mobile apps
2+ years of experience managing product managers
Passion for Lyft and what we are trying to achieve in sustainable urban transportation

Natural ability to make things happen around you. You manage project ambiguity, complexity and interdependencies in an organized and structured way. Be able to define and analyze metrics that inform the success of products
Excellent judgment and thoughtfulness about the dynamics of a product ecosystem
Strong communication skills and intuition for communication strategy at every level -- within teams, within the company, to users, to press, and other audiences
Experience leading post-launch optimizations including developing testing strategies
Ability to conceptualize, manage and prioritize multiple projects for stakeholders
Detail oriented with superior organizational skills -- balancing multiple projects, deadlines, and requests should be second nature to you

scope.surface: Core Rider consumer app — home screen, request flow, pickup experience, in-ride experience, rate & pay; targeted experiences including Lyft Silver for Seniors and Price Lock for commuters; AV extensions
primary_metrics_inferred: Rider conversion and completion through request and purchase flows
Pickup and in-ride experience quality (ratings, complaints)
Retention and engagement across rider segments (seniors, commuters)
Revenue via Price Lock and other monetization features
core_responsibilities: Understand Lyft's strategic and competitive position. Set your team's goals and success metrics that align with Lyft's mission and drive maximum impact based on data analysis, market research and usability studies
Set an incredibly ambitious vision that motivates and inspires your team, peers and leadership and scales equally well across places and situations of all types
Completely own your team's products, existing or new, from ideation, through development, to experiment, launch, growth and maintenance, caring deeply about our users
Lead and align cross functional partners and teams: engineers, designers, analysts, marketers, business partners and operations to drive a shared vision
Communicate clear roadmaps, priorities, experiments and decisions across a wide spectrum of audiences from partner teams to executive level

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($176K-$220K base) plus equity and bonus mentioned
Clear product surface ownership described in concrete detail (home screen, request flow, pickup, in-ride, rate & pay)
Explicit people management scope — manages a team of PMs
High-visibility role: product used by millions of people every day
Hybrid schedule clearly defined (Mon/Wed/Thu in office)
Strong benefits package including 18 weeks paid parental leave
red_flags: No visa sponsorship information stated — unclear for international candidates
Hybrid role requires in-office 3 days per week in San Francisco — no full-remote option
Role spans very broad scope (entire rider journey + AV extensions + segment experiences) which may indicate high complexity and competing priorities

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years requirements, people management scope, and comp range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities and product surface; not stated in JD)
ambiguities: The JD lists no explicit nice-to-haves or preferred qualifications section; all listed requirements treated as must-haves.
Equity is mentioned as 'potential equity offering' but form (RSUs, options) is not specified.

Compensation note says 'San Francisco area' range but also mentions salary ranges vary by working location — could imply other locations exist, though the JD specifies San Francisco headquarters only.
